evidence.clue: Abandoned farms, cold chimneys, and neglected fields appear on the road before the village proper.
Millhaven itself shows no smoke, no lamp-light, and no visible movement when the party sees it from the rise.
Jaina identifies Millhaven as the first major knot where the contamination gathers hard.
Inside the village, meals, wash lines, and household objects are left mid-use, proving daily life stopped abruptly rather than after orderly evacuation.
Mira confirms that villagers died, rose later, and moved out at night toward the north road.
The churn behind the houses and the cleaner north-road spoor confirm large-scale postmortem movement out of the village.

readerKnows: Millhaven fell to the plague, its dead rose after death, and a large number of them marched north out of the village.
povCharacterKnows: Sylvanas now knows what happened to Millhaven in broad operational terms, even though she still does not know what first brought the contamination in.
notes: Resolved as a village-fate question in chapter 3. The source question continues under living-contamination-vector and new-grain-seller.
resolution: Millhaven is the first fully claimed village on this road. The living mostly died in place, the dead rose later, and the village emptied northward.
